translations.text: Is it not pleasant to learn with a constant perseverance and application?
It is indeed a pleasure to acquire knowledge and then to constantly review it.

samples.original: 有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎？

translations.text: Is it not delightful to have friends coming from distant quarters?
And is it not a great pleasure to have friends come from afar to visit you?

samples.original: 人不知而不愠，不亦君子乎？

translations.text: Is he not a man of complete virtue, who feels no discomposure though men take no note of him?
Suppose a man is not known or appreciated by the world, and yet he feels no resentment — is he not indeed a wise and good man?

samples.original: 温故而知新，可以为师矣。

translations.text: If a man will keep constantly reviewing what he has acquired, and keep continually advancing to new attainments, he may be a teacher of others.
A man who can constantly discover new knowledge while reviewing the old is qualified to be a teacher.
My doctrine is that of an all-pervading unity.
The great principle of my teaching is that everything is connected into one.

literal_to_free.0-33: 偏好直译。保留原文语序和字面意思，不增删解释性内容。关键词字面译（学→learn，习→practice）。
literal_to_free.34-66: 混合策略。部分调整句式，保留关键名词的字面意思。
literal_to_free.67-100: 偏好意译。拆解原文意群，用目标语习惯表达重述，可增加衔接词（学→acquire knowledge）。

archaic_to_modern.0-33: 偏好古雅风格。选用拉丁/希腊源大词（perseverance, discomposure），使用完整从句，避免缩写。
archaic_to_modern.34-66: 中性风格。混合学术与日常词汇，标准书面语。
archaic_to_modern.67-100: 偏好现代风格。选用日耳曼源短词（joy, help），使用缩写（it's, don't），可加破折号和口语化表达。
